prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) computed at the GFN2-xTB level (semi-empirical tight-binding) in the gas phase (no implicit solvation), charge 0 multiplicity 1 for the neutral; cation at charge +1 multiplicity 2 and anion at charge −1 multiplicity 2 for the finite-difference N±1 states. Geometry taken as-is from the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz; no re-optimization was performed. Partial charges via the Mulliken scheme (GFN2-xTB). The headline value reported (0.22885456) is f⁺ on Fe (atom 11), which is the most electrophilic site. Fe is simultaneously the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191) and has a negative dual descriptor (−0.030527354), indicating preferential nucleophilic / electron-donating character — consistent with Fe(II) chemistry. The Cp ring carbons show low Fukui indices (~0.0136) and the ring hydrogens moderate values (~0.0635). Both rings are symmetry-equivalent within numerical noise, reflecting the molecule's D5 symmetry. Integrity checks: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000 — both passed; integrity status ok, trustworthy: true. Warnings (verbatim from tool): 'sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)' and 'sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)' — both are routine informational checks that passed.
